title: US Fantasy Transit Network
caption: An "unlimited funds" transit network for North America, generated end-to-end from Census data: a four-tier hierarchy of 135 HSR hubs, 2,266 regional hubs, 1,195 organic metro systems, and a commuter web linking all 33,288 towns to at least two neighbors. Big cities split into boroughs and districts, each anchoring its own metro. Explore the interactive viewer.
alt: US Fantasy Transit Network — auto-generated four-tier network map viewer
label: US Fantasy Transit Network
overlayText: Four-tier fantasy transit network generated from Census data — HSR spine, regional mesh, organic metros, and a commuter web reaching all 33,288 towns. Interactive Leaflet viewer.

title: Veins of the Commonwealth
caption: Massachusetts transportation network rendered as a circulatory system — roads, rail, and waterways layered to reveal the structural logic of how the state moves. GPH946 Computer Cartography final project.
alt: Veins of the Commonwealth — Massachusetts transportation network
label: Veins of the Commonwealth
overlayText: MA transportation network as a circulatory system — roads, rail, and waterways layered to reveal how the state moves.

title: Chronic Absenteeism — Lynn Public Schools
caption: One of 20 maps from my MS capstone: hexbin grid of student absence rates across Lynn, MA. Geocoded student addresses and correlated distance from school with chronic absenteeism. Built in R.
alt: Hexbin absenteeism map — Lynn Public Schools
label: Chronic Absenteeism — Lynn
overlayText: One of 20 maps from my MS capstone. Geocoded student addresses, hexbin grids, KDE heatmaps, and subgroup breakdowns.

title: Growth in the Granite State
caption: Multitemporal Landsat 8/9 change detection over Concord, NH — magenta and green flag where built surfaces replaced vegetation between captures. Pansharpened to 15m; the final composite from the Growth in the Granite State study.
alt: Concord, NH — Landsat multitemporal change detection
label: Growth in the Granite State
overlayText: Landsat 8/9 multitemporal change detection over Concord, NH. Pansharpened to 15m resolution.

title: Salem Pantry: Mapping Food Access
caption: Coverage gap analysis for the Salem Pantry — 150,000+ client records spatially analyzed to identify underserved areas and model statewide similarity populations using random forest.
label: Salem Pantry: Food Access
overlayText: 150,000+ client records analyzed. Coverage gaps mapped, statewide similarity modeled via random forest using Census ACS data.

title: Education & Wealth in Massachusetts
caption: Bivariate choropleth correlating median household income to educational attainment across Massachusetts municipalities. My first GIS project, built in ArcGIS Pro with Census ACS data.
alt: Education and wealth bivariate map — Massachusetts
label: Education & Wealth in MA
overlayText: Bivariate choropleth — median household income vs. educational attainment across MA municipalities. First GIS project.

title: Salem Emergency Evacuation Routes
caption: arcpy-based route planning tool weighting FEMA flood zone data and traffic volume to identify safest and most dangerous evacuation paths from Salem, MA in a sea-rise flood scenario.
label: Salem Evacuation Routes
overlayText: arcpy network analysis weighted by FEMA flood zones and traffic volume — safest evacuation paths in a sea-rise scenario.

title: SSU Central Campus Survey
caption: Salem State University central campus mapped by combining CAD utility drawings, drone orthomosaics, and Trimble GPS ground verification — integrating traditional surveying with modern geospatial methods.
label: SSU Central Campus
overlayText: CAD drawings + drone orthomosaics + Trimble GPS ground-truthing combined into a single campus basemap.

title: Lynnfield Cemetery Spatial Database
caption: ~4,000 grave cards and ~1,000 lot cards digitized, georeferenced, and linked to a SQL geodatabase for Forest Hill and Willow cemeteries in Lynnfield, MA.
label: Lynnfield Cemetery Database
overlayText: 4,000+ grave cards digitized into a queryable SQL geodatabase. Historic cemetery maps georeferenced in ArcGIS Pro.

title: Optimal EV Charging Station Siting
caption: GIS-AHP multi-criteria analysis reviewing global EV deployment literature and proposing a spatial methodology for Massachusetts — covering equity, network analysis, and location-allocation models.
alt: EV charging station siting research map
label: EV Charging Station Siting
overlayText: GIS-AHP MCDM framework for MA EV deployment — reviewing global literature and proposing an equity-aware spatial methodology.

title: Colorado Elevation
caption: Elevation map of Colorado rendered in ArcGIS Pro — digital elevation model visualized with hillshade and classified color ramp. GPH946 Computer Cartography.
label: Colorado Elevation
overlayText: DEM visualized with hillshade and classified color ramp. ArcGIS Pro cartography exercise.

title: New Hampshire Population
caption: Choropleth map of New Hampshire population by county. GPH946 Computer Cartography — one of six maps built to practice thematic mapping in ArcGIS Pro.
label: New Hampshire Population
overlayText: County-level choropleth. Thematic mapping exercise in ArcGIS Pro — classification, symbology, layout.

title: NYC Buildings
caption: New York City building footprints rendered by height or type in ArcGIS Pro. GPH946 Computer Cartography — urban density visualization at the building level.
alt: New York City building footprints map
label: NYC Buildings
overlayText: Building footprint visualization — urban density rendered at the individual structure level in ArcGIS Pro.

title: US Area Codes
caption: One of my first real maps — US telephone area codes by region, working through nominal data classification and multi-category legend design.
label: US Area Codes
overlayText: Early piece — US area codes by region. Where I figured out nominal classification and legend design.

title: US Cities
caption: From the very first batch of maps I ever made — US cities with proportional symbols scaled by population, learning graduated point symbology and visual hierarchy.
alt: United States cities proportional symbol map
label: US Cities
overlayText: Early piece — US cities with proportional symbols. Learning visual hierarchy and graduated point symbology.

title: US States
caption: An early reference piece — state-level US choropleth with classified attribute data and a full cartographic layout (north arrow, scale bar, legend — the works).
label: US States
overlayText: Early piece — state-level US choropleth with the full cartographic layout.

title: US States & Capitals
caption: One of my first real maps — US reference map with state capitals, working through layout, typography, and reference map conventions.
alt: US states and capitals reference map
label: US States & Capitals
overlayText: Early piece — US capitals reference map. Layout, type hierarchy, capital placement.

title: US States & Capitals — Illustrator Refinement
caption: The same GPH946 reference map, lifted out of ArcGIS Pro and finished by hand in Adobe Illustrator — relief-shaded states, labeled oceans and bordering countries, refined typography, and a custom compass mark. The design-side counterpart to the raw GIS export.
alt: US states and capitals — Adobe Illustrator refined cartographic version
label: US States & Capitals — Illustrator
overlayText: The ArcGIS Pro reference map, finished by hand in Adobe Illustrator: relief shading, labeled oceans and neighbors, refined type, and a custom compass mark.

title: Edwards Aquifer — Barton Springs Segment
caption: Structural-geologic map of the Barton Springs segment of the Edwards Aquifer, southwest of Austin, Texas — faulted Cretaceous limestone units, the Mount Bonnell and Barton Springs faults, recharge creeks, and spring outlets. Built during my GPH903 introductory GIS coursework.
alt: Edwards Aquifer Barton Springs segment geologic map near Austin, Texas
label: Edwards Aquifer — Barton Springs
overlayText: Faulted Cretaceous limestone units and recharge features of the Barton Springs segment, southwest of Austin, TX. GPH903 study area.

title: Massachusetts EV Charger Locations
caption: Current EV charging station distribution across Massachusetts — built for a literature review on optimal siting methodology. Part of the GPH904 GIS Research final project.
label: MA EV Charger Locations
overlayText: Current station distribution used as baseline for GIS-AHP optimal siting research. GPH904.

title: Predicted Extreme Heat — Rhode Island
caption: Random forest model output predicting extreme heat event risk across Rhode Island — built in R for GPH942 Advanced Geographic Quantitative Methods. Machine learning applied to spatial climate data.
alt: Rhode Island extreme heat risk prediction map
label: RI Extreme Heat Prediction
overlayText: Random forest model predicting extreme heat risk statewide. R-based machine learning applied to spatial climate data.

title: Optitrek: Olson 2015 vs OR-Tools 2026
caption: Same 50 US landmarks, same Google distance matrix Randy Olson used in his viral 2015 ‘optimal road trip’ — but solved with Google OR-Tools’ constraint-based VRP. Result: 2.32 hours saved off 224 driving hours (~1% improvement). 44 of 50 edges agree between the two solvers; the disagreement concentrates in dense Midwest clusters where small re-orderings shave minutes.
alt: Side-by-side route comparison — Olson 2015 genetic algorithm vs OR-Tools 2026 VRP solver
label: Optitrek: Olson vs OR-Tools
overlayText: Same 50 landmarks, same distance matrix, two different optimizers — OR-Tools’ VRP shaved 2.32 hours off Olson’s genetic-algorithm tour.

title: Optitrek: Four-Way Route Overlay
caption: All four Optitrek routing solutions plotted on the same map — Olson 2015, OR-Tools capped, OR-Tools uncapped, and the OSRM-routed variant. Same 50 stops; the divergence between lines is where solver philosophy and routing engine disagree on the right path between two waypoints.
alt: Four routing solutions overlaid on the same 50-stop tour
label: Optitrek: Four-Way Overlay
overlayText: Four solver/router combinations plotted on the same 50 stops — divergence shows where they disagree.

title: Optitrek: OSRM Western Parks Proof
caption: Validation tour for the Optitrek routing pipeline — eight Western US parks (Yellowstone, Bryce, Zion, Grand Canyon, and more) routed against a self-hosted OSRM US extract. 2,800 miles, 59.3 hours of driving across 8 legs. Proof that the self-hosted router returns geometrically correct paths.
alt: Eight-stop Western US parks loop routed on self-hosted OSRM
label: Optitrek: OSRM Proof
overlayText: 8-stop western parks loop routed on self-hosted OSRM — 2,800 mi / 59.3 hours. Pipeline validation.

title: Lynn: KDE Heatmap of Student Addresses
caption: Kernel density estimate of geocoded student home addresses around Lynn English High School. Darker = higher relative density. Built in R with ggplot2 over a Stadia Maps basemap. One of 20 maps from my MS capstone.
alt: KDE heatmap of student addresses around Lynn English High School
label: Lynn: KDE Heatmap
overlayText: Kernel density of geocoded student home addresses around LEHS — built in R + ggplot2 + Stadia basemap.

title: Lynn: Absenteeism by Ethnicity (Small Multiples)
caption: Six-panel small-multiple comparison of absenteeism distribution by self-reported ethnicity (Asian, Black, Hispanic, Indigenous, Multiracial, White) within 3 square miles of Lynn English HS. Same scale, same filter, same basemap across all six panels — only the data changes.
alt: Six-panel small multiples of absenteeism by ethnicity around Lynn English HS
label: Lynn: Ethnicity Panels
overlayText: Six-panel small multiples — absenteeism distribution by ethnicity within 3 miles of LEHS.

title: Lynn: Citywide Absenteeism
caption: Citywide view of mapped absenteeism for Lynn English HS students — each dot is a student address, colored by their personal absence rate (0–100%). Darker reds indicate students missing more school. R + ggmap with Stadia basemap.
alt: Citywide absenteeism dot map for Lynn English HS students
label: Lynn: Citywide Absenteeism
overlayText: Every LEHS student plotted by home address, colored by personal absence rate. Darker = higher absenteeism.

title: Lynn: Absenteeism by Grade Level
caption: Four-panel small multiples of hexbin absenteeism broken out by grade level (9, 10, 11, 12) — same scale, same filter, same area, only the grade changes. Patterns hold roughly across grades but density shifts subtly between underclassmen and seniors.
alt: Four-panel small multiples of absenteeism hexbins by grade level at LEHS
label: Lynn: Grade-Level Panels
overlayText: Four-panel small multiples — absence rate hexbins by grade (9, 10, 11, 12).

title: Lynn: Absenteeism by Multilingual Learner Status
caption: Side-by-side hexbin comparison of absenteeism patterns between Multilingual Learner (ML) and Non-ML students around Lynn English HS. Maps the geographic dimension of the multilingual learner achievement gap.
alt: Side-by-side hexbin map: ML vs non-ML student absenteeism around Lynn English HS
label: Lynn: ML vs Non-ML
overlayText: Multilingual Learner vs Non-ML absenteeism — the achievement gap rendered geographically.

title: Lynn Public Schools — Study Area
caption: Reference map of every Lynn Public Schools site — elementary, middle, high, charter, vocational, and parochial — inside the Lynn city boundary. Built in ArcGIS Pro using WGS 1984 Web Mercator. The foundation map every other capstone analysis builds on.
alt: Reference map of all Lynn Public Schools sites within the Lynn city boundary
label: Lynn: LPS Study Area
overlayText: Reference map of every LPS site within the Lynn city boundary — foundation for the capstone.

title: Salem Antipodal Ring
caption: Every point on this ring around Salem, MA has a matching antipode on the exact opposite side of the globe. Geometric exploration of antipodal correspondence — output from the Geopuesto playground.
alt: Geometric ring showing antipodal correspondence around Salem, MA
label: Salem Antipodal Ring
overlayText: Antipodal correspondence around Salem, MA — every point on the ring has a matching point on the opposite side of the globe.

title: TappyMaps Demo: US Uninsured Rate
caption: Choropleth demo from TappyMaps, an interactive cartography tool I’m building — state-level US uninsured rates in 5 quantile bins from 2023 Census data. The map renders as a shareable card.
alt: TappyMaps choropleth of US uninsured rate by state
label: TappyMaps: Uninsured Rate
overlayText: State-level US uninsured rate, 5 quantile bins, 2023 Census data — rendered as a TappyMaps share card.

title: Granite State: Full Landsat 8/9 False-Color Composite
caption: Full-resolution multitemporal Landsat 8/9 false-color composite centered on Concord, NH and its surroundings — a zoomed-out view of the same scene as the closer Concord tiles. Green = healthy vegetation, magenta = developed/disturbed surfaces, the contrast pulling urban edges out of the surrounding forest. Source imagery for the Growth in the Granite State change-detection project.
alt: Full-resolution Landsat false-color composite of Concord, New Hampshire and surroundings
label: Granite State: Landsat False-Color
overlayTitle: Granite State: Full Landsat False-Color Composite
overlayText: Multitemporal Landsat 8/9 over greater Concord, NH. Green = vegetation, magenta = developed/disturbed surfaces.

title: Pleiades Multispectral: Melbourne Port
caption: Pleiades-1 multispectral satellite imagery over the Port of Melbourne — side-by-side comparison of multispectral (left, NIR mapped to red so vegetation/heat lights up crimson) and panchromatic (right). The resolution is fine enough to count individual shipping containers in the yard.
alt: Pleiades multispectral satellite image of the Port of Melbourne shipping yards
label: Pleiades: Melbourne Port
overlayText: Multispectral vs panchromatic Pleiades-1 imagery over the Port of Melbourne. Resolution fine enough to count individual shipping containers.

title: Pleiades Multispectral: Melbourne Cricket Ground
caption: Pleiades-1 multispectral imagery centered on the Melbourne Cricket Ground and surrounding sports precinct. Near-infrared is mapped to the red channel — turf and trees burn bright crimson, hard surfaces stay grey. Side-by-side with the panchromatic band for context.
alt: Pleiades multispectral satellite image centered on Melbourne Cricket Ground
label: Pleiades: MCG Sports Precinct
overlayText: NIR-as-red mapping makes turf and trees burn crimson around the MCG. Multispectral (L) vs panchromatic (R).

title: Pleiades Multispectral: Melbourne Wide View
caption: Pleiades-1 multispectral satellite imagery covering the wider Melbourne metropolitan area — Port Phillip Bay, the CBD, and the surrounding suburbs. Multispectral (left) and panchromatic (right) at the same extent for direct comparison.
alt: Wide Pleiades multispectral satellite view of greater Melbourne and Port Phillip Bay
label: Pleiades: Melbourne Wide
overlayText: Wide-area Pleiades-1 imagery of Melbourne, Port Phillip Bay, and surrounding suburbs. Multispectral vs panchromatic.

title: Salem Pantry: Users per Census Block Group
caption: Choropleth of Salem Pantry users aggregated to Census Block Group around Salem, MA. The blue-classed block groups directly adjacent to the pantry carry the bulk of the load.
alt: Salem Pantry: Users per Census Block Group
label: Salem Pantry: Users by CBG
overlayTitle: Salem Pantry: Users per Census Block Group
overlayText: Pantry users per Census Block Group around Salem, MA.

title: Africa — February False Color Composite
caption: Continental-scale false-color composite of Africa for February — Red & Blue channels = visible red, Green channel = infrared. The Sahara, Sahel, and central African forest belts pop out as distinct spectral regions.
label: Africa Feb False Color
overlayText: Continental false-color composite of Africa for February — visible red on R/B, infrared on G.

title: Africa — NDVI Vegetation Index
caption: Continental NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) of Africa computed from February imagery (feb_b1, feb_b2). Range −0.66 to +0.99: deep green = dense vegetation, red = bare / desert.
label: Africa NDVI
overlayText: Continental NDVI of Africa from February imagery. Deep green = dense vegetation, red = bare/desert.

title: Cape Ann False Composite
caption: Finished cartographic layout: Cape Ann (MA) false-color composite using Landsat 8 ETM+ Bands 3, 4, 5 as B, G, R. The vegetation/water/built-up tri-color contrast pulls the coastal land use into focus.
label: Cape Ann FCC
overlayText: Cape Ann (MA) false-color composite — Landsat 8 ETM+ Bands 3,4,5 as B,G,R.

title: Eastern Massachusetts — Coastal Landsat Mosaic
caption: Two Landsat scenes mosaicked and seamlessly edge-matched in GIS into a single wide-extent natural-color image over eastern Massachusetts — Boston metro, the South Shore, Cape Cod, and Buzzards Bay. Subtle clouds drift over the Atlantic.
alt: Eastern Massachusetts — two-scene Landsat mosaic, natural color
label: Eastern MA Landsat Mosaic
overlayText: Two Landsat scenes mosaicked and edge-matched in GIS — Boston metro, South Shore, Cape Cod, Buzzards Bay.

title: Urban Heat Classification — Boston Metro
caption: Classified urban heat island analysis over the Boston metro — categorical raster mapping built/impervious surfaces against vegetated cooler zones. Course exercise output.
label: Boston UHI Classification
overlayText: Classified urban heat island analysis — categorical raster over Boston metro built vs vegetated zones.

title: Lynn: Student Address Distribution (Citywide)
caption: Citywide bubble dot map of LEHS student home addresses. Bubble size = number of students at that address. LEHS marked in blue. R + ggmap with Stadia basemap.
label: Lynn: Student Distribution
overlayText: Citywide bubble dot map of LEHS student home addresses.

title: Lynn: Student Addresses near LEHS (1-Mile Zoom)
caption: 1-mile zoom of LEHS student home addresses. Bubble size = students per address. Tight neighborhood detail around the school.
alt: Lynn: Student Addresses near LEHS (1-Mile Zoom)
label: Lynn: Near LEHS (1 mi)
overlayTitle: Lynn: Student Addresses near LEHS (1-Mile Zoom)
overlayText: 1-mile zoom of LEHS student home addresses with bubble sizing.

title: Lynn: Student Density Grid (150 m cells)
caption: Citywide student density rendered as raw counts per 150-meter square grid cell. Easier to compare neighborhoods than a dot map; reveals cluster structure around Central Square.
alt: Lynn: Student Density Grid (150 m cells)
label: Lynn: Student Density Grid
overlayTitle: Lynn: Student Density Grid (150 m cells)
overlayText: Citywide student density — raw counts per 150m grid cell.

title: Lynn: Absenteeism Hotspots (≥20%) around LEHS
caption: Square-grid hotspot map filtering for cells with average absence rate at or above 20%. Anchored with Lynn neighborhood landmarks (Central Square, Eastern Ave, Lynnway).
label: Lynn: Absence Hotspots ≥20%
overlayText: Square-grid hotspot map of ≥20% absence rate cells around LEHS.

title: Lynn: Absenteeism Dot Map (Close to LEHS)
caption: Close-up dot map — each LEHS student plotted by home address, colored by personal absence rate (0–100%). Darker = higher absenteeism. The pattern around Central Square is striking.
alt: Lynn: Absenteeism Dot Map (Close to LEHS)
label: Lynn: Absence Dots (Close)
overlayTitle: Lynn: Absenteeism Dot Map (Close to LEHS)
overlayText: Close-up dot map of LEHS students colored by personal absence rate.

title: Lynn: Absenteeism Hotspots (Citywide Hexbin)
caption: Citywide hexbin hotspot map of chronic absenteeism — 100-meter hexagons showing only cells with absence rate above 20%. Reveals citywide pattern at a glance.
label: Lynn: Absence Hexbin
overlayText: Citywide hexbin hotspot map filtered to >20% absence rate.

title: Lynn: Absenteeism by SPED Status (Hexbin)
caption: Side-by-side hexbin comparison of absenteeism patterns between Special Education (SPED) and Non-SPED students around LEHS. Same scale, same area — only the population changes.
label: Lynn: SPED vs Non-SPED
overlayText: Side-by-side hexbin of absenteeism — SPED vs Non-SPED students.

title: Salem Pantry: Dominant Access-Point Cluster by Block Group

label: Salem Pantry: AP Clusters
overlayTitle: Salem Pantry: Dominant Access-Point Cluster by Block Group
overlayText: Dominant access-point cluster assignment per CBG around Salem.

alt: Quabbin: Locating the Swift River Valley
label: Quabbin: Locator
overlayTitle: Quabbin: Locating the Swift River Valley

overlayTitle: Quabbin: Four Towns Under the Water

title: Quabbin: Erased From the Map

title: Quabbin: Filling in Equal-Area Stages

overlayText: Small multiples of the reservoir filling to its 1946 full pool.

title: Quabbin: The Aqueduct to Boston

overlayText: The aqueduct carrying the water ~105 km east to Boston.
